title: Command version exit code not zero
body:
### Issue Description

When use the command `podman --version` the exit code get 125 not zero

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Just use the command `podman --version`

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

The command `podman --version` should exit code 0 by success.

comments.body:
Please provide more details, where are you executing this command? How did you install podman,etc..
If it exits with an error code does it print a error message?
Could be running out of memory?

> Please provide more details, where are you executing this command? How did you install podman,etc.. If it exits with an error code does it print a error message?

I'm using C# to call the process and just want get the version information.
The version information can be obtained correctly, but will get the return code 125.

> > Please provide more details, where are you executing this command? How did you install podman,etc.. If it exits with an error code does it print a error message?
> 
> I'm using C# to call the process and just want get the version information. The version information can be obtained correctly, but will get the return code 125.

Then please provide a full reproducer with it. Do you get the some behaviour from your shell?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
As we haven't heard anything back, closing
